commands.review_summary:
The PR canonicalizes LINE webhook paths so a configured trailing slash registers and dispatches through the same route bucket as the no-trailing-slash path.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +37. Total +40 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: configure LINE with `webhookPath: "/line/webhook/"`; current main registers the bucket as `/line/webhook` but the route handler reads `/line/webhook/`, leaving no target for signature validation.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should handle this through the normal PR review, CI, and merge flow.

target.title: fix(line): canonicalize trailing-slash webhook paths
target.body:
## Summary
- Canonicalize the configured LINE webhook path before registering the target and before route lookup, so `/line/webhook/` and `/line/webhook` use the same target bucket.
- Update the LINE lifecycle test mock to mirror SDK trailing-slash canonicalization and add a regression test for signed POST dispatch with a trailing-slash `webhookPath`.
- Add a changelog entry for the user-facing LINE webhook compatibility fix.

## Credit
- Based on the ClawSweeper finding for commit 4cf228466770c9358fb5e7507edaa4b7e7dacb02.

## Verification
- `pnpm test:serial extensions/line/src/monitor.lifecycle.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
